name: clawdcontext
displayName: ClawdContext — Markdown OS for AI Agents
description: VS Code extension for AI coding agent Markdown systems: CER dashboard, mdcc-style diagnostics, lessons governance, SKILL.md security scanning, and quick-fix refactors.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Read-only analysis works in untrusted workspaces. File-generating and refactor commands should be used in trusted workspaces.

commands.title: ClawdContext: Compare CER with Previous Commit

clawdcontext.tokenBudget.description: Total context window size in tokens (e.g. 200000 for Claude Sonnet)

clawdcontext.cerWarningThreshold.description: CER threshold below which a warning is shown (0.0 - 1.0)

clawdcontext.cerCriticalThreshold.description: CER threshold below which a critical alert is shown (0.0 - 1.0)

clawdcontext.lessonsTtlDays.description: TTL in days for local-heuristic lessons before staleness warning

clawdcontext.lessonsMaxEntries.description: Maximum recommended lessons before bloat warning

clawdcontext.alwaysLoadedFiles.description: Files always loaded into context (counted toward CER)

clawdcontext.onDemandPatterns.description: Glob patterns for on-demand files (not counted as always-loaded)

clawdcontext.enableCodeLens.description: Show inline CodeLens on lessons.md entries

clawdcontext.enableStatusBar.description: Show CER and context health in the status bar

clawdcontext.securityAllowlist.description: Security rule codes to suppress (e.g. ["SEC_EXEC_SHELL", "SEC_CRED_ENV"]). Use for first-party skills with legitimate shell commands or env vars.
clawdcontext.securityAllowlist.markdownDescription:
Security rule codes to suppress entirely. Available codes:
- `SEC_EXFIL_FETCH` — HTTP fetch to external URLs
- `SEC_EXFIL_DNS` — DNS exfiltration
- `SEC_INJECT_HIDDEN` — Encoded injection
- `SEC_INJECT_ROLE` — Role override
- `SEC_INJECT_DELIMITER` — Delimiter manipulation
- `SEC_CRED_ENV` — Env var access
- `SEC_CRED_FILE` — Sensitive file access
- `SEC_EXEC_SHELL` — Shell commands
- `SEC_EXEC_EVAL` — Dynamic eval
- `SEC_PERSIST` — Persistence
- `SEC_STEALTH_COMMENTS` — Hidden comments
- `SEC_OBFUSC` — Code obfuscation
- `SEC_RECON` — System reconnaissance

clawdcontext.trustedDomains.description: Domains to trust in URL pattern matching (e.g. ["clawdcontext.com", "github.com"]). URLs matching these domains won't trigger SEC_EXFIL_FETCH.
clawdcontext.trustedDomains.markdownDescription:
Domains to trust for URL-based security patterns. URLs containing these domains are excluded from `SEC_EXFIL_FETCH` findings.

Example: `["mycompany.com", "github.com"]`

clawdcontext.securityCodeBlockAware.description: Suppress security findings inside markdown fenced code blocks (``` ... ```). These are documentation examples, not executable threats.

enumDescriptions: Ollama (local models — no API key needed)
clawdcontext.ai.provider.description: AI provider for optional intelligent features. Set to 'none' to keep the extension fully offline.
clawdcontext.ai.provider.markdownDescription:
AI provider for optional intelligent features.

- `none` — AI disabled (default, fully offline)
- `openai` — [OpenAI API](https://platform.openai.com/)
- `anthropic` — [Anthropic API](https://console.anthropic.com/)
- `ollama` — [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) (local, no API key needed)

clawdcontext.ai.apiKey.description: API key for OpenAI or Anthropic. Not needed for Ollama. Stored in VS Code settings (use Secrets API or env var for production).
clawdcontext.ai.apiKey.markdownDescription:
API key for your AI provider.

- **OpenAI**: starts with `sk-`
- **Anthropic**: starts with `sk-ant-`
- **Ollama**: leave empty (no key needed)

clawdcontext.ai.model.description: Model name override. Leave empty for defaults: gpt-4o-mini (OpenAI), claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (Anthropic), llama3.2 (Ollama).
clawdcontext.ai.model.markdownDescription:
Model name override. Leave empty for smart defaults:

| Provider | Default Model |
|---|---|
| OpenAI | `gpt-4o-mini` |
| Anthropic | `claude-sonnet-4-20250514` |
| Ollama | `llama3.2` |

clawdcontext.ai.baseUrl.description: Custom base URL for the AI API. Useful for enterprise proxies, Azure OpenAI, or self-hosted endpoints. Leave empty for defaults.
clawdcontext.ai.baseUrl.markdownDescription:
Custom base URL override.

Defaults:
- OpenAI: `https://api.openai.com`
- Anthropic: `https://api.anthropic.com`
- Ollama: `http://localhost:11434`

Examples:
- Azure OpenAI: `https://my-resource.openai.azure.com`
- Enterprise proxy: `https://ai-proxy.mycompany.com`

clawdcontext.ai.caCertPath.description: Path to a PEM certificate bundle for enterprise TLS/SSL. Required when behind corporate proxies with custom CA certificates (e.g., ZScaler, Netskope).
clawdcontext.ai.caCertPath.markdownDescription:
Path to a PEM CA certificate bundle file.

Required for enterprise environments with custom CA certificates (e.g., ZScaler, Netskope, Bluecoat).

The certificate is merged with system root CAs so both enterprise and public endpoints work.

Example: `/etc/ssl/certs/enterprise-ca.pem` or `~/certs/corp-ca-bundle.pem`

clawdcontext.ai.rejectUnauthorized.description: Verify TLS certificates. Set to false only for debugging with self-signed certificates. NOT recommended for production.
clawdcontext.ai.rejectUnauthorized.markdownDescription:
Verify TLS/SSL certificates.

⚠️ Set to `false` **only** for debugging with self-signed certs. This disables certificate validation entirely.

For enterprise proxies, prefer `clawdcontext.ai.caCertPath` instead.

clawdcontext.ai.timeout.description: AI request timeout in milliseconds. Increase for large prompts or slow connections.
clawdcontext.ai.timeout.markdownDescription:
AI request timeout in milliseconds.

- Default: `30000` (30 seconds)
- Ollama with large models: consider `60000`+
- Enterprise proxies: consider `45000`+

scripts.check: npm run compile && npm run lint && npm test
scripts.package:ci: npm run compile && npm test && vsce package --no-yarn
